name: Caves and Crosses of the Sierra de la Alfaguara Trail
name_original: Cruz de Alfacar y Cruz de Víznar desde Fuente Grande — circular en el Parque Natural Sierra de Huétor
description: Hike the difficult 5.1-mile Caves and Crosses of the Sierra de la Alfaguara Trail, exploring natural caves and historic crosses with panoram
region_intro: Hiking around Alfacar offers access to the Sierra de Huétor Natural Park, characterized by medium-range mountains, regenerated pine forests, and native evergreen and gall oaks. The landscape features dramatic geological formations, including narrow gorges, sheer cliffs, and numerous springs. The area also encompasses the rugged Sierra de la Alfaguara, with its ravines, peaks, and abundant streams. Best hiking trails around Alfacar The most popular hiking route is Fuente del Fraile-Sanatorio Trail, a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 50 minutes to complete. This route explores historical trenches and the ruins of a sanatorium. Another top favourite among local hikers is Caves and Crosses of the Sierra de la Alfaguara Trail, a difficult 5.1 miles (8.1 km) path. This route offers an immersive experience in the Alfaguara mountains with significant elevation gain. Local hikers also love the Llano del Fraile Trenches – La Yedra Trenches loop from Cerro de las Higuerillas, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail leading through historical trench sites, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around Alfacar is defined by pine forests, geological formations, and ancient springs within the Sierra de Huétor Natural Park. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to challenging treks. The routes in Alfacar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 1100 hikers have used komoot to explore Alfacar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Alfacar?
A: There are over 25 hiking routes documented around Alfacar on komoot, offering a wide range of experiences. These include 3 easy routes, 11 moderate routes, and 11 difficult routes, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Alfacar?
A: Hiking around Alfacar primarily takes you through the Sierra de Huétor Natural Park and the rugged Sierra de la Alfaguara. You'll encounter medium-range mountains, regenerated pine forests, native evergreen and gall oaks, as well as dramatic geological formations like narrow gorges, sheer cliffs, and abundant streams. Some routes also feature historical trenches.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Alfacar?
A: Yes, Alfacar offers several easy routes suitable for families or those seeking a more relaxed walk. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21836487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Cerro Calvero&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.2 km trail with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a leisurely outing.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while hiking in Alfacar?
A: Alfacar's trails are rich in history. You can explore routes that pass by historical trenches, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979694071/fuente-del-fraile-sanatorio-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Fraile-Sanatorio Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which also visits sanatorium ruins. The region is also home to the historically significant Fuente Grande (Aynadamar) spring and the Federico García Lorca Park, commemorating the poet. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21836449" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lorca Memorial Site loop from Alfacar&lt;/a&gt; is another route that connects with this history.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alfacar?
A: Spring is ideal for experiencing the vibrant wildflowers and orchids that carpet the countryside. Autumn is also highly recommended, especially for visiting the Alfaguara Arboretum, where the deciduous trees display spectacular colors. The region generally offers pleasant hiking conditions outside of the hottest summer months.

Q: Can I spot any wildlife on the trails around Alfacar?
A: The diverse habitats of the Sierra de Huétor Natural Park support a variety of wildlife. Hikers may encounter Iberian ibex, wild cats, wild boars, and foxes. The area is also known for its birds of prey, including golden eagles and eagle owls, and a unique insect population, such as the distinctive blue butterfly *Polyommatus Bellargus Subsp Alfacariensis*.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, Alfacar offers several circular routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14602310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llano del Fraile Trenches – La Yedra Trenches loop from Cerro de las Higuerillas&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 11.4 km trail that allows you to return to your starting point without retracing your steps.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Alfacar?
A: The hiking routes in Alfacar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 190 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the natural beauty of the Sierra de Huétor Natural Park, and the opportunity to explore historical sites.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Alfacar?
A: While specific regulations can vary within natural parks, generally, dogs are permitted on trails in the Sierra de Huétor Natural Park, often requiring them to be on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. It's always advisable to check local signage or park guidelines for any specific restrictions.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced trekkers, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351587990/caves-and-crosses-of-the-sierra-de-la-alfaguara-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caves and Crosses of the Sierra de la Alfaguara Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 8.1 km route with significant elevation gain, offering an immersive experience in the rugged Alfaguara mountains. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21836449" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lorca Memorial Site loop from Alfacar&lt;/a&gt;, which also features substantial climbs.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trails in Alfacar?
A: Alfacar, being a town at the edge of the natural park, typically offers various parking options within the town itself or at designated trailheads. Specific parking availability can depend on the starting point of your chosen route. It's recommended to check the route details on komoot for information on parking near the trailhead.
tips: From the magnificent hill dominated by the white cross of Alfacar, you can see an extensive landscape in which Sierra Elvira, Sierra Nevada and the Cubillas reservoir stand out, among others.
The Cruz de Víznar is placed at the top of the Collado de Víznar, indicating the highest peak in the municipality.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is approximately 8.1 kilometers long and generally takes around 3 hours and 26 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your pace, stops for photos, and exploration of the caves or crosses.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this route?
faqs.answer: The Caves and Crosses of the Sierra de la Alfaguara Trail is rated as difficult. It features mountainous terrain with rugged paths, rocky areas, and potentially loose stone. Some sections, especially near the crosses, can be steep and require good fitness and sure-footedness.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter diverse terrain, from reforested pine forests to areas with native oak and gall oak. The limestone geology has created spectacular ravines and cliffs. Expect rugged paths, rocky sections, and potentially loose stone, especially on ascents to viewpoints like the Cruz de Víznar.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is notable for its natural caves, such as Cueva de Gato and Cueva de los Mármoles, and significant historic crosses. You'll pass by the Cruz de Alfacar and the Cruz de Víznar, both offering panoramic views. The area also has historical significance, including traces of trenches from the Spanish Civil War.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route covers sections of several official trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the 'Cuevas y cruces de la Sierra de la Alfaguara' (50% coverage), 'Cruz de Víznar' (32%), 'Cueva del Agua' (23%), 'Itinerario de las Trincheras' (23%), and 'Sendero Barranco de Víznar' (15%).

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: While the Sierra de Huétor Natural Park generally allows dogs, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the rugged terrain and potential wildlife encounters. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a difficult hike and carry enough water for both of you.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times for hiking in the Sierra de la Alfaguara, offering pleasant temperatures. Summer can be very hot, while winter may bring snow and icy conditions, particularly at higher elevations and around the crosses, making the trail more challenging and potentially hazardous.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the natural park?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Caves and Crosses of the Sierra de la Alfaguara Trail or to enter the Sierra de Huétor Natural Park. However, always respect park regulations regarding waste, fire, and wildlife.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts near Fuente Grande in Alfacar. While specific parking information isn't detailed, you can usually find parking in or near the village of Alfacar, which serves as a common starting point for routes in this area.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see on the trail?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de la Alfaguara is home to diverse wildlife. Keep an eye out for wild boars, goats, badgers, and weasels. The area is also rich in bird species, making it a great spot for birdwatching.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and rugged terrain, it's essential to bring sturdy hiking boots, plenty of water, snacks, sun protection, and appropriate layers for changing weather. A map or GPS device is also recommended, as some sections can be less defined. Trekking poles can be helpful for stability on rocky or steep parts.
